exercises.name: Push-Up
steps: Start in a high plank position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Keep your core engaged, back flat, and body in a straight line from head to heels.
Lower your body by bending your elbows until your chest is just above the floor.
Push through your hands to extend your arms and return to the starting position.

exercises.name: Pull-Up
steps: Grip the pull-up bar with an overhand grip slightly wider than shoulder-width.
Hang freely with your arms fully extended and your core engaged.
Pull your body upward by driving your elbows down and back toward the floor.
Continue pulling until your chin clears the bar.
Lower yourself back down with control to the starting position.

exercises.name: Chin-Up
steps: Grip the bar with an underhand grip (palms facing you), spaced shoulder-width apart.
Hang freely with your arms extended.
Pull yourself up by driving your elbows down while squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Pause when your chin is above the bar.
Slowly lower yourself back to a full hang.

exercises.name: Dips
steps: Grip the parallel bars and jump up to a support position with your arms fully extended.
Lean your torso slightly forward to target the chest, or stay upright to target the triceps.
Bend your elbows to lower your body until your shoulders are below your elbows.
Push back up to the starting position by straightening your arms.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Squat
steps: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and your toes pointed slightly outward.
Keep your chest up and your core braced.
Push your hips back and bend your knees to lower your body as if sitting in a chair.
Descend until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor.
Drive through your heels to stand back up to the starting position.

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squat
steps: Stand a couple of feet in front of a bench or chair, facing away from it.
Place the top of one foot on the bench behind you.
Keep your torso upright and lower your hips until your front thigh is parallel to the floor.
Ensure your front knee does not collapse inward.
Push through your front heel to return to the starting position.

exercises.name: Plank
steps: Place your forearms on the floor with your elbows aligned below your shoulders.
Extend your legs straight behind you, resting on your toes.
Squeeze your glutes and brace your core.
Hold your body in a straight line from your head to your heels without letting your hips sag.

exercises.name: Glute Bridge
steps: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor, hip-width apart.
Place your arms at your sides with palms facing down.
Squeeze your glutes and push through your heels to raise your hips toward the ceiling.
Pause at the top when your body forms a straight line from your knees to your shoulders.
Slowly lower your hips back to the floor.

exercises.name: Walking Lunge
steps: Stand tall with feet together and hands on your hips.
Take a large step forward with your right leg.
Drop your back knee straight down until it almost touches the floor, keeping your front shin vertical.
Push off your right foot to bring your feet back together.
Repeat by stepping forward with your left leg.

exercises.name: Pike Push-Up
steps: Start in a downward dog position, with hips high in the air and hands shoulder-width apart.
Keep your legs straight and shift your weight forward onto your hands.
Bend your elbows to lower your head toward the floor between your hands.
Push back up to the starting inverted-V position.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
steps: Lie flat on the bench with your feet firmly planted on the floor.
Grip the barbell slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Unrack the bar and hold it straight over your chest with arms fully extended.
Inhale and lower the bar slowly until it lightly touches your mid-chest.
Exhale and press the bar forcefully back to the starting position.

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Press
steps: Set an adjustable bench to an incline of 30 to 45 degrees.
Sit on the bench with a dumbbell in each hand resting on your thighs.
Kick the weights up to your shoulders and lean back.
Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended.
Lower the dumbbells under control back to shoulder level.

exercises.name: Barbell Overhead Press
steps: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and grip the barbell just outside your shoulders.
Unrack the bar and rest it across your upper chest/collarbone.
Brace your core and squeeze your glutes.
Press the bar straight up overhead until your arms are locked out.
Slowly lower the bar back to your upper chest.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
steps: Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand by your sides, palms facing inward.
Keep a slight bend in your elbows and brace your core.
Raise your arms straight out to the sides until the dumbbells are at shoulder height.
Pause for a moment at the top.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to your sides.

exercises.name: Barbell Bent-Over Row
steps: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell with an overhand grip.
Hinge at your hips and push your glutes back until your torso is nearly parallel to the floor.
Keep your back straight and let the bar hang below your shoulders.
Pull the bar toward your lower ribs by driving your elbows up and back.
Lower the bar slowly to the starting position.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Single Arm Row
steps: Place your left knee and left hand on a flat bench to support your body.
Hold a dumbbell in your right hand with your arm extended toward the floor.
Pull the dumbbell up toward your hip, keeping your elbow tucked close to your body.
Squeeze your back muscles at the top.
Lower the dumbbell slowly to the starting position and repeat.

exercises.name: Barbell Back Squat
steps: Step under the barbell in a squat rack, resting it across your upper back.
Unrack the bar and take a step back, positioning your feet shoulder-width apart.
Take a deep breath, brace your core, and push your hips back to initiate the squat.
Lower yourself until your hip crease is below your knees.
Drive forcefully through your legs to stand back up, exhaling at the top.

exercises.name: Barbell Front Squat
steps: Rest the barbell across your front deltoids and clavicle, supporting it with your fingers or crossed arms.
Keep your elbows high and chest up.
Squat down by breaking at the hips and knees until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
Drive up through your heels to return to the starting position.

exercises.name: Goblet Squat
steps: Hold a single dumbbell or kettlebell vertically against your chest with both hands.
Stand with your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Squat down, pushing your knees out, keeping your chest up and the weight close to your body.
Go down until your elbows touch your inner knees.
Push through your feet to stand back up.

exercises.name: Barbell Deadlift
steps: Stand with your mid-foot under the barbell, feet hip-width apart.
Bend at the hips and knees to grab the bar with a grip just outside your legs.
Flatten your back, lift your chest, and engage your lats.
Drive through your feet and pull the bar up along your legs by extending your hips and knees simultaneously.
Stand tall at the top, then lower the bar by pushing your hips back and bending your knees once the bar passes them.

exercises.name: Sumo Deadlift
steps: Take a very wide stance with your toes pointed outward.
Drop your hips and grip the bar with your hands inside your legs.
Keep your chest up and back flat.
Drive through your feet, extending your hips and knees to lift the bar.
Lock out at the top, then carefully reverse the movement to lower the weight.

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlift (RDL)
steps: Stand tall holding a barbell or dumbbells at hip level with an overhand grip.
Keep your legs mostly straight with only a slight bend in the knees.
Push your hips back and hinge forward, lowering the weight straight down your legs.
Go down until you feel a deep stretch in your hamstrings (usually just below the knees).
Squeeze your glutes and thrust your hips forward to return to the standing position.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lunge
steps: Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand by your sides.
Take a large step forward with one leg and lower your hips until both knees are bent at a 90-degree angle.
Keep your torso upright and ensure your front knee does not track far over your toes.
Push off your front foot to return to the starting position.
Alternate legs with each rep.

exercises.name: Barbell Hip Thrust
steps: Sit on the floor with your upper back resting against a flat bench and a padded barbell over your hips.
Plant your feet flat on the floor, about hip-width apart.
Drive through your heels and thrust your hips upward until your body forms a straight line from your shoulders to your knees.
Squeeze your glutes hard at the top.
Lower your hips back down under control.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bicep Curl
steps: Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides, palms facing forward.
Keep your upper arms pinned to your sides and your shoulders back.
Curl the dumbbells upward toward your shoulders by flexing your elbows.
Squeeze your biceps at the top of the movement.
Lower the weights slowly back to the starting position.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Hammer Curl
steps: Stand holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with a neutral grip (palms facing your legs).
Keep your elbows tucked into your sides.
Curl the dumbbells up toward your shoulders, maintaining the neutral grip.
Pause and squeeze at the top.
Lower the dumbbells under control to the starting position.

exercises.name: EZ-Bar Curl
steps: Stand holding an EZ-curl bar with an underhand grip.
Keep your elbows locked at your sides.
Curl the bar up toward your chest, contracting your biceps.
Slowly lower the bar back to the starting position.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Overhead Tricep Extension
steps: Sit or stand holding one dumbbell with both hands, extending your arms straight up over your head.
Keep your elbows pointed upward and close to your head.
Bend your elbows to lower the dumbbell behind your head until you feel a stretch in your triceps.
Extend your arms back to the top position.

exercises.name: Lying Triceps Extension
steps: Lie on a flat bench holding an EZ-bar or dumbbells directly above your chest with arms extended.
Keep your upper arms stationary.
Bend your elbows to lower the weight down toward your forehead.
Extend your elbows to press the weight back to the starting position.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Shrugs
steps: Stand holding a dumbbell in each hand by your sides.
Keep your arms straight and elevate your shoulders toward your ears in a shrugging motion.
Hold the contraction at the top for a second.
Slowly lower your shoulders back to the neutral position.

exercises.name: Cable Tricep Pushdown
steps: Attach a rope or straight bar to a high cable pulley.
Grip the attachment and stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, bracing your core.
Keep your elbows tucked securely against your sides.
Push the attachment down until your arms are fully extended.
Control the weight as it returns to chest height.

exercises.name: Overhead Cable Tricep Extension
steps: Attach a rope to a high or medium pulley.
Turn your back to the machine, grabbing the rope behind your head.
Step forward to create tension, leaning slightly forward.
Extend your arms out and up until they are straight.
Slowly return your hands behind your head.

exercises.name: Cable Face Pull
steps: Set a cable pulley to upper-chest or face height and attach a rope.
Grip the rope with both hands and step back to create tension.
Pull the rope directly toward your face, letting your elbows flare outward and upward.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together as the rope splits past your ears.
Slowly return to the starting position with arms extended.

exercises.name: Seated Cable Row
steps: Sit at a low cable row station and place your feet on the platforms, keeping a slight bend in your knees.
Grip the V-handle attachment and sit up tall with your back straight.
Pull the handle toward your stomach by driving your elbows straight back.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the peak of the contraction.
Slowly extend your arms to return the weight, getting a good stretch in your lats.

exercises.name: Cable Crossover
steps: Set two pulleys at a high position facing each other.
Grab a handle in each hand and step forward into a staggered stance.
Keep a slight bend in your elbows and your chest up.
Bring your hands together in a downward arc in front of your chest.
Slowly open your arms back to the starting position, feeling a stretch in your chest.

exercises.name: Cable Woodchopper
steps: Set a cable pulley to the highest position and attach a single D-handle.
Stand sideways to the machine with your feet shoulder-width apart and grip the handle with both hands.
Rotate your torso and pull the handle diagonally down and across your body toward your opposite hip.
Keep your arms relatively straight throughout the movement.
Slowly return to the starting position, controlling the rotational pull of the cable.

exercises.name: Cable Lateral Raise
steps: Set a cable pulley to the lowest position and attach a single D-handle.
Stand sideways to the machine and grab the handle with your outside hand.
Keeping your arm slightly bent, raise it outward and upward until it is parallel to the floor.
Lower the handle slowly back down to your side.

exercises.name: Cable Bicep Curl
steps: Attach a straight bar or rope to a low pulley.
Stand facing the machine, holding the attachment with an underhand grip.
Keep your elbows pinned to your sides.
Curl the weight up to your upper chest.
Lower the weight back down slowly.

exercises.name: Straight Arm Pulldown
steps: Attach a straight bar or rope to a high pulley.
Stand facing the machine, step back slightly, and grab the bar with straight arms.
Hinge forward slightly at the hips.
Keeping your arms straight, push the bar down in an arc until it hits your thighs.
Slowly let the bar rise back to face level.

exercises.name: Cable Pull-Through
steps: Attach a rope to a low pulley. Stand facing away from the machine, straddling the cable.
Bend at the hips and reach between your legs to grab the rope.
Walk forward a few steps to create tension.
Keeping your back straight, hinge at the hips to let the cable pull your hands through your legs.
Squeeze your glutes and push your hips forward to stand back up straight.

exercises.name: Leg Press
steps: Sit down in the leg press machine and place your feet on the sled at shoulder-width.
Release the safety catches and press the sled up until your legs are extended (do not lock out your knees).
Lower the sled slowly by bending your knees until they form a 90-degree angle.
Press forcefully through your heels to return the sled to the top.

exercises.name: Leg Extension
steps: Sit in the leg extension machine with your back flat against the pad.
Position the padded roller so it rests just above your ankles.
Grip the handles by your sides to keep your body anchored.
Extend your legs fully to lift the weight, squeezing your quadriceps at the top.
Lower the weight slowly back to the starting position.

exercises.name: Seated Leg Curl
steps: Adjust the machine so that the pivot point aligns with your knees and the pad rests behind your lower calves.
Lower the lap pad so it securely holds your thighs in place.
Curl the padded lever backward and downward by flexing your hamstrings.
Pull as far as you can comfortably go.
Control the weight as you slowly extend your legs back to the start.

exercises.name: Lying Leg Curl
steps: Lie face down on the leg curl machine with the roller pad resting on the back of your ankles.
Grip the handles under the bench for stability.
Curl your legs upward, bringing your heels as close to your glutes as possible.
Squeeze your hamstrings at the top, then slowly lower the weight back down.

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown
steps: Sit at a lat pulldown machine and secure your knees under the thigh pads.
Grip the wide bar with an overhand grip outside shoulder-width.
Lean back slightly and pull the bar down toward your upper chest.
Squeeze your lats and shoulder blades together at the bottom of the movement.
Slowly let the bar back up until your arms are fully extended.

exercises.name: Machine Chest Fly
steps: Adjust the seat height so the handles are at chest level.
Sit back against the pad, grip the handles, and keep a slight bend in your elbows.
Pull the handles together in front of your chest in a smooth, hugging motion.
Squeeze your chest muscles hard at the center.
Slowly open your arms wide to return to the starting position.

exercises.name: Machine Reverse Fly
steps: Sit facing the pad of the pec deck machine (reverse of the chest fly).
Grip the horizontal handles with your arms slightly bent.
Pull the handles backward by squeezing your rear delts and upper back.
Bring the handles as far back as comfortable, then return to the start.

exercises.name: Smith Machine Squat
steps: Position the Smith machine bar across your upper back and shoulders.
Unlatch the bar and step your feet slightly forward compared to a traditional barbell squat.
Lower your hips down and back until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
Push through your feet to stand back up.
Re-rack the bar securely when finished.

exercises.name: Hack Squat
steps: Step into the hack squat machine, placing your shoulders under the pads and your back against the rest.
Place your feet shoulder-width apart on the platform.
Release the safety handles and lower your body by bending your knees to 90 degrees.
Push through your heels to return to the starting position.

exercises.name: Machine Chest Press
steps: Sit in the chest press machine with your back flat against the pad.
Grip the handles at chest level.
Press the handles forward until your arms are fully extended.
Slowly let the handles return to the starting position.

exercises.name: Machine Shoulder Press
steps: Adjust the seat so the handles align with your shoulders.
Sit with your back straight against the pad and grip the handles.
Press the weight straight up over your head until your arms are fully extended.
Slowly lower the handles back to shoulder level.

exercises.name: Seated Calf Raise
steps: Sit on the machine, placing the balls of your feet on the footplate.
Adjust the thigh pads so they rest snugly just above your knees.
Lift the weight slightly and disengage the safety lever.
Lower your heels as far as possible to stretch the calves, then push up onto your toes as high as you can.
Squeeze at the top, then slowly lower back down.

exercises.name: Standing Calf Raise
steps: Stand with your shoulders under the machine pads and the balls of your feet on the elevated block.
Drop your heels down toward the floor to stretch the calves.
Press up onto your toes as high as possible.
Pause and squeeze your calves, then lower back down slowly.

exercises.name: Hip Abductor Machine
steps: Sit in the machine with your legs inside the padded levers.
Select your weight and sit back against the pad.
Push your legs outward against the pads as far as you comfortably can.
Slowly control the return to the starting position.

exercises.name: Hip Adductor Machine
steps: Sit in the machine and place your legs outside the padded levers.
Adjust the machine so your legs are spread wide to start.
Squeeze your thighs together against the resistance until the pads meet in the middle.
Slowly let your legs spread back apart to the starting position.

exercises.name: Treadmill Run
steps: Step onto the treadmill and set a desired speed and incline.
Begin running or jogging at a steady pace, maintaining an upright posture.
Keep your arms relaxed and pumping naturally at your sides.
Monitor your heart rate and breath, maintaining a sustainable pace for your fitness level.

exercises.name: Stationary Bike
steps: Adjust the seat height so your knees are slightly bent when pedaling at the bottom.
Set a resistance level that challenges you without compromising form.
Begin pedaling at a steady, controlled pace, keeping your core engaged.
Maintain consistent breathing and monitor your heart rate throughout the duration.

exercises.name: Rowing Erg
steps: Adjust the foot straps and damper setting to your preference, then sit on the seat.
Grasp the handle with both hands and keep your arms extended with a slight knee bend.
Drive your legs while pulling the handle toward your torso, then extend your legs again.
Maintain a rhythmic pace with consistent breathing throughout your workout.

exercises.name: Elliptical
steps: Step onto the elliptical and grip the stationary or moving handles.
Set your desired resistance and incline level.
Move your legs in a smooth, elliptical motion while coordinating your arm movement.
Maintain an upright posture and steady cadence for the duration of your session.

exercises.name: Stair Climber
steps: Stand on the stair climber with your hands lightly gripping the handrails.
Set a desired speed and difficulty level.
Climb the stairs at a steady pace, focusing on pushing through your heels.
Maintain an upright posture and control your breathing throughout.

exercises.name: Jump Rope
steps: Hold the rope handles at hip height with your elbows bent at about 90 degrees.
Jump on the balls of your feet, turning the rope overhead in one smooth motion.
Keep your torso upright and maintain a steady rhythm.
Continue jumping at a pace that matches the rope speed, breathing steadily throughout.

exercises.name: Burpees
steps: Start standing with your feet hip-width apart.
Squat down, place your hands on the floor, and jump or step your feet back into a plank position.
Perform a push-up, then jump or step your feet back toward your hands.
Explode upward, jumping into the air with your arms overhead, then return to the starting position.

exercises.name: Mountain Climbers
steps: Start in a high plank position with your hands shoulder-width apart.
Drive one knee toward your chest while keeping the other leg extended.
Quickly switch leg positions in a running motion, maintaining a steady pace.
Keep your core engaged and hips level throughout the movement.

exercises.name: High Knees
steps: Stand tall with your feet hip-width apart.
Run in place while driving your knees up toward chest height.
Pump your arms in rhythm with your leg movement.
Maintain a quick, steady pace and control your breathing.

exercises.name: Jumping Jacks
steps: Stand with your feet together and arms at your sides.
Jump upward while spreading your feet and raising your arms overhead.
Land softly on the balls of your feet with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Jump again to return to the starting position and repeat in a continuous motion.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Swing
steps: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, a kettlebell on the floor in front of you.
Hinge at the hips and grab the kettlebell with both hands, keeping your chest up.
Drive your hips forward explosively to swing the kettlebell up to about chest height.
Let gravity bring the kettlebell back down through your legs, repeating the hip drive in a continuous motion.

exercises.name: Goblet Squat (KB)
steps: Hold a kettlebell vertically against your chest with both hands, elbows bent.
Stand with your feet slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Squat down by pushing your hips back, keeping your chest up and weight in your heels.
Drive through your feet to stand back up, keeping the kettlebell close to your body.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Clean
steps: Place the kettlebell on the floor between your feet.
Hinge at the hips and swing the kettlebell up with one arm, loading your hips.
As the bell rises, punch your fist upward and rotate your elbow under the handle.
Catch the bell in the racked position at shoulder height, then set it down and repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Kettlebell Snatch
steps: Stand with the kettlebell on the floor slightly in front of one foot.
Swing the kettlebell up with power, driving your hips forward as you pull with your arm.
As the kettlebell reaches chest height, rotate your elbow under and punch the bell overhead in one fluid motion.
Lock out your arm with the kettlebell overhead, then lower it back down following the same path.

exercises.name: Turkish Get-Up
steps: Lie on your back with one knee bent and the kettlebell pressed overhead in one arm.
Drive through your heel to roll onto your forearm, then place your hand on the ground.
Push through your legs to transition into a half-kneeling position, keeping the kettlebell stable.
Stand up fully while keeping the kettlebell pressed overhead, then reverse the movement to return to lying.

exercises.name: KB Front Rack Carry
steps: Clean the kettlebell to your shoulder in the racked position.
Stand tall with your core braced and engaged throughout the movement.
Walk forward for a prescribed distance or time, maintaining upright posture.
Keep the kettlebell stable at shoulder height, breathing steadily as you move.

exercises.name: KB Single-Arm Row
steps: Stand with your feet hip-width apart, holding a kettlebell in one hand.
Hinge forward at the hips and support yourself with your other hand on a bench or knee.
Pull the kettlebell up toward your hip, driving your elbow back and squeezing your back muscles.
Lower the kettlebell with control and repeat for reps, then switch sides.

exercises.name: KB Overhead Press
steps: Clean the kettlebell to your shoulder in the racked position.
Brace your core and keep your glutes tight.
Press the kettlebell straight overhead until your arm is fully extended.
Lower the kettlebell back to your shoulder with control and repeat.

exercises.name: Band Pull-Apart
steps: Stand upright holding a resistance band with both hands at chest height, arms extended.
Keep a slight bend in your elbows throughout the movement.
Pull the band apart by moving your hands outward in a controlled arc.
Squeeze your rear delts and upper back muscles, then slowly return to the starting position.

exercises.name: Band Chest Press
steps: Anchor the band at chest height behind you, or wrap it around a secure post.
Grab both ends of the band and face away from the anchor point.
Press the band forward until your arms are fully extended.
Slowly return to the starting position with the band at chest level.

exercises.name: Band Lateral Walk
steps: Place a resistance band above your knees or around your calves.
Squat into a quarter-squat position with your knees slightly bent.
Take controlled steps to the side while maintaining tension on the band.
Step in one direction for reps, then repeat in the opposite direction.

exercises.name: Band Bicep Curl
steps: Stand on the center of the band with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Hold the ends of the band with your palms facing forward and arms extended.
Curl the band up toward your shoulders by bending your elbows.
Squeeze your biceps at the top, then lower the band with control.

exercises.name: Band Tricep Pushdown
steps: Anchor the band at head height and face the anchor point.
Grab the band and bring it down to chest height with your elbows bent.
Press the band downward until your arms are fully extended.
Control the band as it returns to chest height.

exercises.name: Band Face Pull
steps: Anchor the band at face height and face the anchor point.
Grab the band with both hands, arms extended in front of your face.
Pull the band toward your face, spreading it apart as you pull.
Squeeze your rear delts and upper back, then slowly return to the starting position.

exercises.name: Band Glute Kickback
steps: Wrap the band around one leg above the knee and hold the other end for stability.
Stand on one leg and keep a slight bend in the standing knee.
Extend the banded leg backward and upward, squeezing your glute at the top.
Lower the leg with control and repeat for reps, then switch sides.

exercises.name: Box Jump
steps: Stand facing a plyo box at a distance of about 1-2 feet away.
Bend your knees and arms in preparation, then explosively jump onto the box.
Land softly on the balls of your feet with your knees slightly bent.
Stand up fully on the box, then step down carefully (do not jump back down).

exercises.name: Jump Squat
steps: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
Lower into a squat position by bending your knees until your thighs are parallel to the floor.
Explosively drive through your heels to jump upward as high as possible.
Land softly on the balls of your feet, absorbing the impact, then immediately squat and repeat.

exercises.name: Broad Jump
steps: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and your arms at your sides.
Swing your arms backward and bend your knees in preparation.
Explosively drive forward and upward, swinging your arms forward for momentum.
Land on both feet as softly as possible, ensuring a stable landing position.

exercises.name: Jumping Lunge
steps: Start in a lunge position with one foot forward and one foot back.
Lower your hips until both knees are bent at 90 degrees.
Explosively jump upward, switching your leg positions in mid-air.
Land softly in the opposite lunge position and immediately repeat the jump.

exercises.name: Plyo Push-Up
steps: Start in a high plank position with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Lower your body until your chest is just above the floor.
Explosively push upward with enough force to lift your hands off the ground.
Land softly with your hands back on the ground and immediately lower for the next rep.

exercises.name: Tuck Jump
steps: Stand with your feet hip-width apart and your arms at your sides.
Bend your knees slightly and explosively jump upward.
While airborne, drive your knees up toward your chest and pull your toes toward your shins.
Land softly on the balls of your feet with bent knees, then immediately repeat.

exercises.name: Power Clean
steps: Start with the bar at mid-thigh level, feet hip-width apart.
Extend your hips explosively while shrugging and pulling the bar up.
As the bar reaches chest height, rotate your elbows under it and catch it on your shoulders.
Stabilize the position, then lower the bar back to the hang position.

exercises.name: Clean and Jerk
steps: Start with the bar on the floor and perform a clean, catching it on your shoulders.
From the front rack position, dip by bending your knees slightly.
Drive upward powerfully and press the bar overhead, splitting your feet if necessary.
Recover by bringing your feet back together, then lower the bar back to your shoulders.

exercises.name: Snatch
steps: Stand with the bar on the floor, feet hip-width apart, with a wide grip.
Drive through your legs and explosively extend your hips, pulling the bar upward.
As the bar reaches chest height, rotate your elbows under and catch it overhead in a squat position.
Stand up fully, then lower the bar back down in a controlled manner.

exercises.name: Hang Clean
steps: Hold the bar at hip height with your feet hip-width apart.
Extend your hips explosively while shrugging and pulling the bar upward.
Rotate your elbows under the bar as it reaches chest height and catch it on your shoulders.
Stabilize the position, then lower the bar back to hip height.

exercises.name: Push Press
steps: Stand with the bar on your shoulders at eye level, feet hip-width apart.
Bend your knees slightly and dip your hips downward in preparation.
Drive upward forcefully through your legs while pressing the bar overhead.
Lock out your arms overhead, then lower the bar back to shoulder level.

exercises.name: Hanging Leg Raise
steps: Hang from a pull-up bar with your hands shoulder-width apart and arms fully extended.
Engage your core and lift your legs by raising your knees toward your chest.
Continue raising until your legs are parallel to the ground or higher if possible.
Lower your legs slowly with control back to the starting position.

exercises.name: Russian Twist
steps: Sit on the floor with your knees bent and feet slightly elevated off the ground.
Lean back at about a 45-degree angle, keeping your chest up.
Clasp your hands together and rotate your torso to one side, bringing your hands toward the floor.
Rotate to the other side in a controlled manner, maintaining your leg position.

exercises.name: Ab Wheel Rollout
steps: Kneel on the floor and grip an ab wheel with both hands.
Keep your core braced and roll the wheel forward, extending your body toward the floor.
Stop before your chest touches the ground, then pull the wheel back to the starting position.
Keep your hips level throughout the movement.

exercises.name: Bicycle Crunch
steps: Lie on your back with your knees bent and hands placed lightly behind your head.
Lift your shoulders off the ground by engaging your abs.
Bring your right elbow toward your left knee while extending your right leg.
Alternate sides in a pedaling motion, maintaining tension on your abdominals.

exercises.name: Dead Bug
steps: Lie on your back with your arms extended toward the ceiling and your knees bent at 90 degrees.
Engage your core and lower your right arm overhead while extending your left leg toward the floor.
Return to the starting position, then repeat on the opposite side.
Alternate sides in a controlled manner, keeping your lower back pressed to the floor.

exercises.name: Hollow Hold
steps: Lie on your back with your arms extended overhead and your legs straight.
Engage your core and press your lower back into the floor.
Raise your shoulders and legs slightly off the ground, creating a slight arch in your body.
Hold this position while maintaining constant tension, breathing steadily.

exercises.name: Side Plank
steps: Lie on your side with your forearm on the ground, elbow aligned below your shoulder.
Place your top foot on top of your bottom foot, or stagger them for more stability.
Engage your core and lift your hips off the ground, creating a straight line from head to heels.
Hold this position while breathing steadily, then repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Cable Crunch
steps: Set a cable pulley to the high position and attach a rope.
Kneel facing away from the machine with the rope behind your head.
Crunch forward by contracting your abs, bringing your head toward your knees.
Squeeze your abs at the bottom, then slowly return to the starting position.

exercises.name: Preacher Curl
steps: Sit at a preacher bench and place your arms on the pad, elbows on the bench.
Hold a dumbbell or barbell with an underhand grip in each hand.
Curl the weight upward by bending your elbows, keeping them on the bench.
Squeeze your biceps at the top, then lower the weight with control.

exercises.name: Concentration Curl
steps: Sit on a bench with your legs spread wide and a dumbbell in one hand.
Rest your elbow on your inner thigh, arm hanging straight down.
Curl the dumbbell up toward your shoulder by flexing your bicep.
Lower the dumbbell with control back to the starting position.

exercises.name: Cable Glute Kickback
steps: Attach an ankle strap to a low cable pulley on one side.
Face the machine and hold the support bar with both hands.
Extend one leg backward and upward, squeezing your glute at the top.
Lower your leg with control back to the starting position, then repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Front Raise
steps: Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides, palms facing backward.
Keep a slight bend in your elbows and raise both dumbbells forward and upward to shoulder height.
Pause briefly at the top, then slowly lower the dumbbells back to your sides.

exercises.name: Barbell Upright Row
steps: Stand holding a barbell with a narrow grip (about 8 inches apart) at hip level.
Keep your chest up and pull the barbell straight up toward your chin.
Lead with your elbows and keep them high throughout the movement.
Lower the barbell back down to hip level with control.

exercises.name: Reverse Pec Fly (Dumbbell)
steps: Sit at the edge of a bench and hold a dumbbell in each hand, arms extended downward.
Hinge forward at the hips until your torso is nearly parallel to the floor.
Raise the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc until they reach shoulder height.
Squeeze your rear delts, then lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Fly
steps: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, arms extended above your chest.
Keep a slight bend in your elbows and lower the dumbbells in a wide arc toward the floor.
Stop when you feel a stretch across your chest, then bring the dumbbells back to the starting position.
Squeeze your chest muscles at the top.

exercises.name: Farmer's Carry
steps: Stand tall holding a dumbbell or kettlebell in each hand at your sides.
Brace your core and engage your grip strength.
Walk forward for a prescribed distance or time, maintaining an upright posture.
Keep your shoulders back and core tight throughout the movement.
